cardiometabolic_tracker v0.4.0

Event-log panel hardening + v0.3.0 review fixes · deployed 2026-06-12 · pxbuilder-aomrani.canvasmedical.com · commits af289e7 (impl) on 21b6b38 (v0.3.0 acceptance) · SDK 0.163.1
pytest 179 collected · 0 skipped · 179 mocked · 0 live live browser checks 47/47 console triage: 0 plugin-attributable errors download tier shipped: Blob + anchor (Tier 1) Gate 2: Observation has no method/device — column ships "Not recorded"

Highlights

  • The Shift+D overlay is now safe to stumble into. It was an undismissable developer overlay labeled "Pipeline Diagnostics" — words that can read as clinical inside an EHR. It is now a clearly-labeled "Plugin event log — technical support only. Not for clinical use." with a close button, Esc dismissal, a true Shift+D toggle, and focus returned to the chart on close.
  • One-click support report. A Download button produces cardiometabolic_tracker_log_<timestamp>.json — schema-versioned, carrying the patient FHIR ID only (never name or DOB), launch target, browser identity, and every pipeline event with a normalized UTC timestamp. Built by a pure, fully unit-tested Python function; the browser only fills its own timestamp/user-agent and appends its runtime events.
  • Timestamps finally agree. Python pipeline events were stamped in naive local time while JS events were UTC — worthless for support correlation. All timestamps are now UTC with a Z suffix, fixed at the source.
  • Read-only Weight data view. A two-state toggle inside the panel renders the exact observations the chart consumed — capture time (local display, full ISO on hover), weight, Δ from baseline, %TBWL — with the sparse-data caveat mirrored in. No new data path, no extra queries.
  • Both v0.3.0 review findings closed: the export now stamps the clinician's local date (was UTC), and the validation-error page HTML-escapes all interpolated values — one path carried observation-entered text into an unescaped string in a frame that inherits the EHR origin.

Screenshots

Bugs found & fixed

  • R2 (v0.3.0 review finding 2, security): _render_error interpolated error strings into HTML unescaped; convert_weight_to_lbs feeds observation-entered unit text into that path, and about:srcdoc inherits the EHR parent origin. Fixed with html.escape on every value + a mocked test asserting markup renders inert.
  • R1 (v0.3.0 review finding 1): export date used toISOString().slice(0,10) — the UTC date, off by one near midnight outside UTC. Now built from local date parts; pinned by template-scan test and checked live.
  • Module shadowing caught during R2: the handler used a local variable named html, which would have made the new import html raise UnboundLocalError in _render_error. Locals renamed.
  • Timestamp divergence (D2): Python events e.g. 12T10:36:34.456523 (naive local) vs JS 12T15:36:34Z. Fixed at the recording source (_now_iso), not by guessing offsets at render time.

Observations & architectural findings

  • Blob + anchor downloads work inside the about:srcdoc iframe (Chromium, headless and headed): Playwright's download event fires and the file lands with the suggested name. The print-pipeline and copyable-<pre> fallbacks were never needed — recorded as the shipped tier.
  • Gate 2 verdict: SDK 0.163.1 Observation exposes patient, is_member_of, category, units, value, note_id, name, effective_datetime + audit fields — no method, no device (verified against SDK source on disk and docs.canvasmedical.com/sdk/data-observation). The source/method column therefore renders "Not recorded" universally rather than inferring; revisit if a later SDK adds the fields.
  • UTC-Z is transport-only. Audited every _loaded_at / processed_at / pipeline timestamp: none render clinician-facing; the only consumers are the technical log and the support report, where UTC-Z is the spec. Display surfaces (table date column) convert to local in the browser.
  • Schema knowledge stays server-side. The template contains no schema key literals (template-scan test enforces it); JS fills exactly generated_at, user_agent, and entry appends. Future transports (email/direct-to-support) can reuse build_log_export unchanged.

Future version considerations (v0.4.1 candidates)

  • Host-origin entries are currently unreachable in production reports: everything the panel records is plugin-origin by construction. A window.onerror/unhandledrejection hook classifying host-script failures into the log would make the host origin earn its place in the taxonomy.
  • R1 cross-midnight evidence is structural, not observed: the live run happened while local == UTC date. A TZ=Pacific/Kiritimati rerun of the Gate 5 script would observe the decisive case.
  • Event-log growth: entries append per component render; a long-lived chart with many debug refreshAll() calls would grow the list unboundedly. Harmless today (panel is session-scoped), worth a cap if the log gains host events.
  • Canvas CLI 0.166.0 upgrade deliberately deferred mid-cycle (manifest pins sdk_version 0.163.1) — schedule as a between-cycles chore with a re-run of the suite.

Tooling & skill notes

  • The v0.3.0 Tier-0 script's login/iframe machinery was reused wholesale for Gate 5 — the findPluginFrame + openChart helpers are now proven across three cycles and belong in the shared tools/ folder rather than per-session copies.
  • Real keyboard delivery into the srcdoc iframe (click to focus, then page.keyboard) exercised the actual Shift+D/Esc handlers — preferable to synthetic dispatchEvent, which would not have caught focus bugs.
  • Playwright's waitForEvent('download') + acceptDownloads validates the full Blob download path headlessly, including the suggested filename — no manual download step needed.
